topic: An artist discovers someone has tattooed their work
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery lighting felt suddenly too harsh, exposing every minute detail of the canvas. I leaned closer to the abstract piece, my breath catching in my throat. It was a swirling vortex of deep indigo and electric silver, a piece I’d poured weeks of sleepless nights into. Then, my eyes snagged on it. Beneath a particularly luminous streak of cyan, there was a line—a perfectly rendered, intricate tattoo, undeniably human in its design. My hand, which had been resting near the frame, froze mid-air. A coldness began to creep up my spine, a sudden, sharp recognition that made the air feel thin. I took a hesitant step back, my heart hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird. Who would do this?

[story 2]

I traced the edge of the charcoal sketch with a fingertip, my focus narrowed to the texture of the paper. It was supposed to be mine, raw and unadulterated. But as I rotated the print under the gallery lights, a detail emerged that sent a jolt through me. Embedded within the depiction of the storm clouds was a delicate, stylized script, clearly inked onto the surface. My knuckles went white where I gripped the edge of the display case. I pulled my hand away as if burned, my gaze darting around the room, searching for the source of the intrusion. A tremor started in my hands, and I had to consciously steady my breathing to keep from shaking.

[story 3]

"Impossible," I whispered, the sound barely audible over the muted chatter of the opening night. I circled the sculpture slowly, my boots making soft, hesitant sounds on the polished floor. The centerpiece, a portrait rendered in oil and shadow, held my attention captive. As I moved into the direct light, I saw it—a complex pattern, undeniably permanent, woven into the very fabric of the painted subject's features. A wave of intense unease washed over me, making my vision swim slightly at the edges. I stopped dead, my posture stiffening, every muscle in my body tensing in sudden, sharp anticipation of what I was witnessing.
